cycle_stage.rationale: ISM PMI at 53.3 shows expansion, with neither the yield curve nor unemployment flashing late-cycle strain.

narrative: Regime read: Cautiously Neutral (composite score -0.00 on a -1 to +1 scale). Finance conditions are roughly balanced (-0.05), economic data is roughly balanced (+0.09), and market psychology leans roughly balanced (-0.05), with the economy in an Expansion phase.

Finance: the yield curve is positively sloped (+0.37pp), the S&P 500 trades at 28.6x earnings against a ~16.5x historical average, and 4 of 5 tracked sectors are positive over the past month — the classical efficient-market / factor-based baseline this dashboard's behavioral overlay adjusts. Economics: ISM PMI is at 53.3 (expansion), CPI is running 3.5% YoY against a 3.63% Fed funds rate, and unemployment sits at 4.2%. ISM PMI at 53.3 shows expansion, with neither the yield curve nor unemployment flashing late-cycle strain. (standard business-cycle framing — sentiment reads differently depending on where the cycle stands.)

Psychology isn't pulling the read away from fundamentals right now — the fundamentals-only baseline (+0.01) and the full psychology-adjusted composite (-0.00) are essentially the same. Psychology is calm enough that it's only weighted 17% of the final read (vs. a floor of 15% and a ceiling of 55% when conditions get extreme).

No single extreme-positioning signal (herding, loss-aversion-driven hedging, overconfidence, or a Minsky-style extended-calm streak) is flashing right now — sentiment, hedging demand, and volatility all look within a normal range.
